label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question asks which statement is wrong according to the news provided. First, I'll read through the given text again to pick out the relevant details. The article talks about the Columbia accident in 2003, where the shuttle broke up during re-entry, killing all seven astronauts. Then, there's a comparison to the Challenger disaster in 1986, which exploded on take-off. The key points related to the options are: A) The article mentions President Bush saying, "America's space program will go on." So this suggests the US will not give up the program, making option A incorrect if the question states that they will give up. However, the question is asking which is wrong, so if A says they will give up, but the text says they will continue, then A is wrong. B) The article says NASA launched an investigation and it was too early to nail down a precise cause. So B says NASA hasn't found the cause, which is correct according to the text. Therefore, B is correct and not the wrong answer. C) The article says, "In 42 years of US' human space flight, there had never been an accident in the descent to Earth or landing. Challenger exploded just after take-off." So before Columbia, no shuttles had exploded during landing. Therefore, C is correct and not the wrong answer. D) The article states, "Take-off and re-entry into Earth's atmosphere are the most dangerous parts of a space mission."
